description: Admin UX/UI Improvements — fix bugs, polish UI, complete user management, add global search, and restructure navigation around organizations

userStories.title: Fix Unicode encoding in elevator fields
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see proper Swedish characters in the elevator edit form so that field labels and values are readable.
acceptanceCriteria: All field labels display correct Swedish characters (å, ä, ö, Å, Ä, Ö) — no \uXXXX sequences visible anywhere
Identify root cause: check reference data values in Convex database and the import pipeline for escaped Unicode
If data-layer: write a Convex migration to decode existing escaped values
If rendering: fix the component to properly decode Unicode strings
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The encoding issue is in modernization and emergency phone sections. Check reference data values stored in Convex — likely stored as escaped Unicode from CSV/Excel import. Also check packages/utils/excel-import/ for where encoding might be lost.

userStories.title: Fix back button navigation from elevator edit
userStories.description: As a user, I want the back button on the elevator edit page to return me to the page I came from.
acceptanceCriteria: Back button uses router.history.back() or navigates to elevator detail page (/hiss/:id)
If the user came from a list page, back returns them there
Back button no longer hardcodes navigation to /sok
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The back arrow is in apps/admin/src/routes/_authenticated/hiss.$id.redigera.tsx. Currently navigates to /sok. Use router.history.back() or fallback to /hiss/$id.

userStories.title: Debounce search on users page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the search on the users page debounced so it doesn't trigger a server request on every keystroke.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input is debounced (300-500ms delay before querying)
No Convex query fires while user is actively typing
Results appear after user stops typing
Clearing the search field immediately shows all users
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: apps/admin/src/routes/_authenticated/admin.anvandare.tsx. Currently passes searchText directly to useQuery(api.userAdmin.list, { search: searchText }). Add a useDebouncedValue hook or similar.

userStories.title: Move sidebar toggle inside sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar collapse toggle inside the sidebar so it doesn't float disconnected when collapsed.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar toggle button is inside the sidebar component (app-sidebar.tsx), not in the header
Toggle is visible in both expanded and collapsed states
Toggle icon clearly indicates expand/collapse direction
Remove SidebarTrigger from the header layout (_authenticated.tsx)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: SidebarTrigger is currently in _authenticated.tsx layout header. Move it into app-sidebar.tsx, positioning it near the top of the sidebar.

userStories.title: Show user name and email in expanded sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my name and email in the sidebar footer when expanded so I know which account I'm logged in as.
acceptanceCriteria: Expanded sidebar footer shows user avatar, full name, and email
Collapsed sidebar shows only the avatar icon (no text)
Text truncates gracefully if name/email is long
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The sidebar footer currently shows only a UserButton avatar. Use the useSidebar() hook to detect expanded/collapsed state and conditionally render text.

userStories.title: Edit user details dialog
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to edit a user's name, email, role, and organization so I can keep user information up to date.
acceptanceCriteria: Each user row has an edit button (pencil icon)
Clicking edit opens a dialog pre-filled with current name, email, role, organization
Admin can change any field: name, email, role, organization
Changing role from 'customer' to 'admin' clears the organization field
Changing role to 'customer' requires selecting an organization
Save calls api.userAdmin.update and refreshes the list
Validation: email format, required fields
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/admin/src/routes/_authenticated/admin.anvandare.tsx. The backend api.userAdmin.update already exists. Reuse the create dialog pattern for the edit dialog.

userStories.title: Deactivate and delete users
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to deactivate or delete users so I can manage access to the system.
acceptanceCriteria: Each user row has a dropdown menu (three dots) with 'Deactivate' and 'Delete' options
Deactivate toggles the user's active field with confirmation dialog
Deactivated users show a visual indicator (grayed out, 'Inactive' badge)
Delete shows confirmation dialog with the user's name
Delete permanently removes the user via Convex mutation
Cannot delete yourself (button disabled or hidden for current user)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: May need to create new Convex mutations: api.userAdmin.deactivate and api.userAdmin.delete if they don't exist. Check existing backend first. Blocking deactivated users from login (Clerk enforcement) can be noted as a follow-up if it requires Clerk API integration.

userStories.title: Manage user organization membership
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to assign users to organizations and manage their membership in the edit flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit dialog (from US-006) shows organization dropdown populated from api.organizations.list
Organization is required for 'customer' role, hidden for 'admin' role
Org filter dropdown on the users page correctly filters by organization
Changing a user's organization takes effect immediately
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This builds on the edit dialog from US-006. If the org dropdown is already implemented in US-006, this story focuses on ensuring the org filter on the users list page works correctly and the role/org validation is solid.

userStories.title: Reference data page - replace cards with table
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see reference data values in a compact table instead of large cards so I can scan more items without scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace card list with a data table: columns = Value, Status (active/inactive badge), Actions
Actions column contains: Edit (pencil), Merge (if active), Deactivate/Activate toggle
Table supports sorting by value name
Search filter still works as before
Inactive items shown with reduced opacity or strikethrough
'Nytt värde' button remains in the same position
Category dropdown selector remains unchanged
Active/inactive counts still displayed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/admin/src/routes/_authenticated/admin.referensdata.tsx. Reuse the same table pattern from the users page (React Table). Keep existing create/edit/merge dialogs — just change the list rendering from cards to table rows.

userStories.title: Elevator edit form - tabbed layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the elevator edit form organized into tabs instead of a long scrolling page.
acceptanceCriteria: Form is organized into 4 tabs using the existing Tabs component from @elevatorbud/ui
Tab labels: Grundinfo, Teknik, Underhåll & Modernisering, Övrigt
Grundinfo tab: Organization + Section 1 (Identifiering)
Teknik tab: Section 2 (Teknisk specifikation) + Section 3 (Dörrar och korg) + Section 4 (Maskineri)
Underhåll & Modernisering tab: Section 5 (Besiktning och underhåll) + Section 6 (Modernisering)
Övrigt tab: Section 7 (Nödtelefon) + Section 8 (Kommentarer)
Active tab persisted in URL search params (e.g., ?tab=teknik) so refreshing stays on the same tab
Form state preserved when switching tabs (no data loss)
Save button always visible regardless of active tab (sticky footer or outside tabs)
All existing form validation still works
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The edit form sections are already extracted into feature components (BasicInfoSection, TechnicalSpecsSection, etc.) from the feature-slices refactor. This story wraps them in tabs. File: apps/admin/src/routes/_authenticated/hiss.$id.redigera.tsx. Use the Tabs component from @elevatorbud/ui (Radix-based, supports 'line' variant).

userStories.title: Fix maintenance page layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Skötselföretag section on the maintenance page to have proper layout so the chart and table don't feel disconnected.
acceptanceCriteria: Bar chart and matrix table are stacked vertically (chart on top, table below) OR given proper responsive breakpoints
Matrix table is horizontally scrollable with sticky first column (company name)
Section has a clear heading and consistent spacing with other maintenance sections
On smaller screens, both components stack vertically
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: apps/admin/src/features/maintenance/components/maintenance-companies.tsx (or similar — check the feature slices structure). The maintenance companies section has a bar chart and a matrix table competing for horizontal space.

userStories.title: Create global search Convex query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend query that searches across elevators and organizations for the global search feature.
acceptanceCriteria: New Convex query: convex/search.ts with a 'global' query function
Searches elevators by: hissnummer, address, organization name, district
Searches organizations by: name
Returns results grouped by type: { elevators: [...], organizations: [...] }
Results limited to top 10 per type for performance
Search is case-insensitive
Empty query returns empty results (no full table scan)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check if Convex search indexes exist on the elevators and organizations tables. If not, add them. Use Convex's built-in search index feature or filter-based approach depending on what's available. Update convex-api.d.ts in admin app with the new query.

userStories.title: Build command palette UI in header
userStories.description: As a user, I want a global search in the header that opens a command palette (Cmd+K) to search for elevators and organizations.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the org selector dropdown from the header
Add a search input/trigger in the header that opens a command palette (Cmd+K shortcut)
Command palette uses the global search query from US-012
Results grouped by type: 'Hissar' and 'Organisationer' sections
Selecting an elevator navigates to /hiss/:id
Selecting an organization navigates to /admin/organisationer/:id (route may not exist yet — use placeholder)
Search input is debounced (300ms)
Empty state shows helpful text when no results
Keyboard navigation: arrow keys to move, Enter to select, Escape to close
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use cmdk (shadcn command component) or similar command palette pattern. The org selector to remove is in org-selector.tsx or similar component used in the header layout. The org details route (/admin/organisationer/:id) will be created in US-014 — for now just wire up the navigation path.

userStories.title: Organization details page with overview tab
userStories.description: As an admin, I want an organization details page with an overview showing key stats.
acceptanceCriteria: New route: /admin/organisationer/$id (TanStack Router file-based routing)
Page header shows organization name and basic info
Three tab structure: Översikt (active by default), Hissar, Användare
Översikt tab shows: total elevators count, elevators by type (pie or bar chart), upcoming inspections count, maintenance company breakdown
Create necessary Convex queries for org-scoped stats (or reuse existing with org filter)
Back button navigates to organizations list
Organization list page (admin.organisationer.tsx) links each org row to this details page
Active tab persisted in URL search params
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Route file: apps/admin/src/routes/_authenticated/admin.organisationer.$id.tsx. For the overview stats, check if existing analytics queries (api.elevators.analytics.stats, api.elevators.analytics.chartData) accept an org filter parameter — if so, reuse them. Hissar and Användare tab content will be added in US-015 and US-016.

userStories.title: Org details - Hissar tab with sub-views
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see all elevators for an organization with Register, Modernisering, and Underhåll sub-views.
acceptanceCriteria: Hissar tab on org details page shows elevators belonging to this organization
Sub-tabs or view switcher for: Register, Modernisering, Underhåll views
Each view shows org-scoped data matching the corresponding top-level page
Clickable elevator rows navigate to /hiss/:id
Reuse existing feature components (register-table, modernization components, maintenance components) with org filter
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-014 (org details page shell). Reuse the already-extracted feature components from features/register/, features/maintenance/, features/modernization/. Pass the org ID as a filter prop. May need to update Convex queries to accept an optional orgId parameter.

userStories.title: Org details - Användare tab
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see and manage users belonging to an organization from the org details page.
acceptanceCriteria: Användare tab on org details page shows users belonging to this organization
Table columns: Name, Email, Role, Last login
Actions: edit (opens edit dialog from US-006), deactivate (from US-007)
Reuse user management components from the users page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-014 (org details page shell) and US-006/US-007 (user management components). Reuse the edit dialog and action menu from the users page. Filter users by organization_id matching the current org.

userStories.title: Remove OrgProvider and add org column to tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the global org context and show organization info directly in elevator tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove OrgProvider / global org context from the app layout
Remove useSelectedOrg() usage from all pages
Top-level Register page shows ALL elevators across all orgs (no org filter)
Top-level Modernisering page shows all modernization data across all orgs
Top-level Underhåll page shows all maintenance data across all orgs
Each elevator row in tables shows an 'Organisation' column with the org name
Organization name in table rows is clickable, navigates to /admin/organisationer/:id
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is a significant change. Grep for useSelectedOrg and OrgProvider to find all usage sites. Update Convex queries to not require orgId (or make it optional). The org-selector component can be deleted. Make sure the org details page (US-014) and global search (US-013) are in place first as alternatives to the org selector.

userStories.title: Restructure dashboard as landing page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dashboard to be a landing page with links to organizations, recent activity, and shortcuts.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard is no longer org-scoped

Shows: links to organizations (top orgs by elevator count), recent activity (last 10 elevator updates), quick stats (total elevators, total orgs, upcoming inspections), shortcuts to common actions (create elevator, import, reference data)
Organization links navigate to org details page
Clean, professional layout with cards/sections
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-017 (OrgProvider removed). The dashboard route file is likely apps/admin/src/routes/_authenticated/index.tsx or similar. May need new Convex queries for recent activity and cross-org stats.
